description_long: This object is a vintage folding bellows camera, featuring a black textured rectangular body with a prominent accordion-style bellows extending from the main housing. The front of the camera displays a circular lens assembly surrounded by various adjustment knobs and levers, likely for focusing and aperture control. Metal struts and rails support the bellows and provide structural stability, while a handle is visible on the top for portability. The rear section is a solid, boxy compartment, likely housing the film or photographic plate, with a hinged door for loading. The camera's exterior is primarily finished in black, with metallic accents and a slightly glossy surface. Its design is characteristic of early 20th-century portable cameras, often used by professional and amateur photographers in urban environments.
description: A vintage folding bellows camera with a black textured body, accordion-style bellows, and metallic adjustment components. The camera features a prominent lens assembly and a sturdy, portable design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the extended bellows, metal struts, and part of the lens assembly, with the textured body and handle visible.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the solid, rectangular back compartment with a hinged door and no visible lens or bellows.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the full length of the bellows, metal supports, and the camera's boxy housing.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the circular lens assembly, adjustment knobs, and the open bellows framed by metal rails.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
